rulings.Case Title: OLIVER ARMSTRONG VS. FLYWHEEL TAXI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Application For Joinder In Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication; Declaration Of Aaron Hancock
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, September 6, 2024, Line 3. 2 - DEFENDANT SHUMET WUDUE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Application For Joinder In Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication. The court grants Shumet Wudue's partial joinder to the motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication. The motion for summary judgment/adjudication is denied as to causes of action one for negligence and two for false imprisonment. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to causes of action three for conversion and four for fraud. The reasons for these rulings are explained in the companion motion. Wudue did not seek summary adjudication on the claim for punitives. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party (plaintiff) is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, September 6, 2024, Line 18. PLAINTIFF NANCY LOPEZ's MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Off calendar for lack of courtesy copies. SF Local Rule 2.7B. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Case Title: JESUS AGUILAR SALMERON VS. AC STONE GROUP PTY LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, September 6, 2024, Line 19. 2 - DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Defendant's motion to strike is denied as to the punitive damages allegations. As noted, plaintiff pleads a viable concealment claim that can support exemplary damages. The claim is alleged with sufficient particularity. (City of Sanger v. Superior Court (1992) 8 Cal.App.4th 444 [punitive damages could be recovered in toxic tort case].) The court grants the motion to strike without leave to amend as to page 43, line 2 of the complaint ["and other silica-containing stone products to be identified during the course of discovery"]. The allegation is improperly vague, does not identify a product, and does not support a concealment claim under Jones v. ConocoPhillips Co. (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1187, 1200 [plaintiff to identify product causing harm]. (See also Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, 81 ["[t]he law cannot tolerate lawsuits by prospecting plaintiffs who sue multiple defendants on speculation that their products may have caused harm over time through exposure to toxins in them, and who thereafter try to learn through discovery whether their speculation was well-founded."].) If plaintiff discovers additional products from defendant that allegedly harmed him, then he can seek leave to amend. Friday's Law & Motion Calendar will be called out of Dept. 301. Anyone intending to appear in person should report to Dept. 301. However, anyone intending to appear remotely should use the regular Zoom information for Dept. 302's Law & Motion Calendar for 9:30 a.m. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party (plaintiff) is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RCE)

rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Friday, September 6, 2024, Line 19. 1 - DEFENDANT COSTCO WHOLESALE CORPORATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's fourth cause of action for concealment is overruled. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.)
